name: German-Style Mustard
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Black or brown mustard seed (heaping)
recipeInstructions:
Adapted from Gift Ideas from the Kitchen
1. In a small bowl, combine mustard seed and dry mustard.
2. In a 1- to 2-quart non-aluminum pan, combine remaining ingredients: water, cider vinegar, chopped onion, brown sugar, salt, garlic, cinnamon, allspice, and tarragon.
3. Simmer uncovered on medium heat until reduced by half, about 10-15 minutes.
4. Pour the mixture into a blender or food processor with the mustard seed and dry mustard mixture. Puree until desired texture is reached (at least 3 or 4 minutes). Some prefer whole seeds remaining; others prefer a smooth paste.
5. The mixture will thicken considerably upon standing. If it becomes too thick after a few days, stir in additional vinegar.
6. Scrape the pureed mustard into clean, dry jars and cover tightly. Age for at least 3 days before using.
